definitions: The act or process of adding or embedding an Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) file or data into a larger model, system, or workflow.
A specific instance where IFC data is integrated into a project, often referring to the point of integration within a Building Information Modeling (BIM) process.
examples: The IFC insertion into the master model improved data interoperability.
We need to verify the accuracy of the IFC insertion before proceeding.
Successful IFC insertion is crucial for collaborative design.
